title: Laws of Motion and Friction - Study Notes

sections.heading: Basic Principles of Laws of Motion
sections.content: Newton's laws of motion govern the relationship between a body and the forces acting upon it, and its motion in response to those forces.
formulas: True weight w = √(w1 w2) for a balance with unequal arms
Apparent weight in ascending lift = m(g + a_0)
Apparent weight in descending lift = m(g - a_0)
Force due to fluid flow: F = ρAv²
specialCases: If a balance has equal arms, true weight w = (w1 + w2) / 2
If power P ∝ v³, then if v becomes n times, P becomes n³ times.

sections.heading: Friction
sections.content: Friction is the force resisting the relative motion of solid surfaces, fluid layers, and material elements sliding against each other.
formulas: Limiting static friction, F_lim = μ_s N
Angle of friction θ: tan θ = μ_s
Angle of Repose α: tan α = μ_s
Acceleration down a rough inclined plane: a = g(sin θ - μ_k cos θ)
specialCases: Pulling is easier than pushing because pushing increases the normal reaction (N = mg + F sin θ) whereas pulling decreases it (N = mg - F sin θ).

sections.heading: Connected Bodies and Pulleys
sections.content: Analysis of masses connected by strings passing over pulleys.
formulas: Acceleration of Atwood machine (two masses m1 > m2): a = ((m1 - m2) / (m1 + m2)) g
Tension in Atwood machine: T = (2 m1 m2 / (m1 + m2)) g
